job_description:
About the job
The future you want is within reach. Let’s build it together.

At PCL Construction, Inc., part of the PCL Family of Companies (PCL), we don’t just build projects—we build opportunities, careers and communities. We are 100% employee-owned, every employee has a stake in our success, and that shared commitment drives how we work, grow and lead in the construction industry.

We’re a team of builders who care deeply about what we create and who we build it with. That includes you. We are not only investing in what’s next in construction, we are investing in what’s next for your career.

As a Estimating Data Analyst for PCL Construction, Inc. in Tempe, Arizona, you’ll have the opportunity to leverage data analytics and technology to enhance the accuracy and efficiency of our estimating processes. In this role, you will analyze large datasets, develop and refine cost estimation models, and create visual dashboards and reports that translate complex data into actionable insights for project teams and leadership. Using tools such as SQL, Excel, PowerBI, Python, and AI platforms, you will help identify trends, optimize workflows, and support data-driven decision-making across our estimating and project teams.

Why Choose PCL?

Choose a career with rewards that matter. PCL’s total rewards are designed to support your growth, well-being and future success—because when you succeed, we all do.

Our Offerings Could Include

Employee ownership opportunities that build long-term value
Annual discretionary performance bonuses
401(k) with company match
Industry-leading medical, dental and vision benefits
Prescription drug coverage and telemedicine services
Life, AD&D and disability insurance
Paid parental leave and family care support
HSA or FSA for healthcare, dependent care and transportation
Mental health and wellness support, including Employee Assistance Programs
Career growth pathways, leadership development and mentorship programs
Access to world-class training through PCL’s College of Construction and professional development courses
Ongoing opportunities to learn new skills, explore different roles and grow your career across sectors and regions

Here's how a Estimating Data Analyst for PCL Construction, Inc. within Civil West contributes to our team:

Responsibilities

Supports estimators on preparing bids and proposals.
Develop, maintain, and refine cost estimation models using historical data, trends, and statistical techniques.
Create dashboards and reports to present trends and projections to leadership and owners.
Utilize AI to optimize estimating/administrative processes.
Assists with bid document control and documenting, organizing, and archiving all information relating to the estimate for successful transfer to the project team. 
Develops and maintains strong relationships with all stakeholders including lead estimator, lead bid runner, subtrade partners and suppliers. 

Qualifications

Background: degree in (mathematics, statistics, computer science, data analytics, AI)
Technical skills in some or all of the following: SQL, Excel, PowerBI, Python, Tableau, Azure (SQL server), Co-Pilot (or other AI platforms)
Strong critical thinking skills
Ability to analyze large data sets and translate into actional business insights
Ability to communicate technical findings to non-technical clients
Accuracy and attention to detail
Ability to represent complex data in visual dashboards

Your Work Has Purpose Here

PCL projects are where life happens, where communities connect, careers begin, and progress is made. Regardless of the type of project, we are building something bigger: a future that's inclusive, resilient and full of opportunity.

At PCL Construction, Inc., we are committed to creating a workplace where everyone belongs. We value the diverse experiences, identities and perspectives our employees bring. Employment decisions are based on merit, potential and the drive to make a difference, regardless of race, gender, age, ability or background.

We know everyone's needs are different, if you require accommodation during the application process, please contact careers@pcl.com and include the position and location of interest.

Company: PCL Construction, Inc.

Primary Location: Tempe, Arizona

Job Title: Estimating Data Analyst

end

policy_result.mobility_strategy: Focus on Canadian employers utilizing the Global Skills Strategy for expedited processing and US-based companies with remote-first global hiring entities. Prioritize mid-to-large scale enterprises that have established internal legal infrastructure for H-1B or TN visa sponsorship.
priority_policy_themes: National AI investment and enterprise AI adoption
constraints: Remote-friendly teams expand the opportunity set
opportunity_signals: Canadian tech hubs like Toronto and Vancouver offer favorable pathways for international talent in AI-driven analytics.
Growth-oriented industries such as climate-tech and healthcare infrastructure are increasingly hiring remote analysts to support data-heavy operations.
Leverage your specific experience in disaster analytics and biomedical research as niche differentiators for firms navigating high-stakes regulatory environments.
policy_result.explanation: Given the lack of current work authorization, the strategy shifts from a broad search to a targeted approach focusing on regions with accessible visa pathways like Canada. By aligning your background in disaster analytics and biomedical research with growth-oriented sectors, you increase your value proposition for firms that prioritize specialized technical expertise. Targeting remote-friendly roles effectively removes geographical friction, allowing you to access a wider pool of employers capable of handling international sponsorship.

entry_barriers: Requires domain-specific understanding of clinical data
ranked_industries.long_term_growth: Durable demand and strategic data problems ensure long-term career stability.

application_assets_result.cover_letter_draft: I am writing to express my strong interest in the Estimating Data Analyst position at PCL Construction. My background in predictive modeling and analytics-heavy internships has prepared me to modernize estimating workflows and improve bid accuracy. I am particularly drawn to PCL’s commitment to employee-owned growth and am eager to apply my technical proficiency in SQL, Python, and PowerBI to drive operational efficiency. I am confident that my ability to bridge the gap between complex datasets and leadership decision-making will make me a valuable asset to your team.

I have been following PCL’s work in construction estimating and am very impressed by your focus on data-driven bid modernization. I would love to learn more about how your team is leveraging analytics to refine cost estimation and whether my profile aligns with your current needs.
application_assets_result.networking_message: Hi, I am Lee, an early-career Data Analyst passionate about operational optimization in high-stakes industries. I am currently exploring paths in construction tech and admire the work being done at PCL. I would appreciate the opportunity to hear your perspective on what skills and projects are most valued for analysts looking to make a direct impact on project estimation and bid accuracy.
application_assets_result.why_this_role_answer: This role is the perfect intersection of my technical skills in SQL and Python and my passion for data-driven operational optimization. I am drawn to PCL’s culture of employee ownership and the opportunity to apply my predictive modeling experience to modernize construction estimating workflows, directly contributing to the accuracy and efficiency of major infrastructure projects.

Passionate about operational optimization, predictive modeling, and building high-integrity dashboards that drive leadership decisions. Currently seeking opportunities to apply technical rigor to solve complex estimation and workflow challenges.
application_assets_result.explanation: Application assets serve as the tangible evidence that bridges the gap between your raw technical skills and the specific business problems a hiring manager needs to solve. By transforming abstract capabilities into reusable artifacts like dashboards and forecasting models, you convert your potential into a verified, low-risk investment for the employer.
